response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior full-stack/web engineer with a strong software craftsmanship background, but their profile has essentially no overlap with the core requirements of this role. There is no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, GPU or accelerator architecture work, or deep-learning workload performance analysis. While they meet the years-of-experience threshold with a BS in Computer Science, the domain gap between web/product engineering and principal-level silicon performance architecture is too large to justify an interview.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Low-latency systems experience (DLang, async I/O, fibers) handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and technical community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator experience whatsoever
No deep neural network training/inference optimization or SIMD/MIMD architecture background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from principal-level hardware performance architecture
